example: The perspicacious detective noticed clues that others had overlooked.
mnemonic: per(통하여) + spic(보다, 라틴어 specere) + acious = 꿰뚫어 보는 → 통찰력 있는. spec(보다): inspect, spectacle

example: A judicious use of resources will ensure the project stays within budget.
mnemonic: judic(판단하다) + ious = 판단을 잘하는 → 현명한. jud(판단): judge, judicial, adjudicate

example: The philosopher's abstruse arguments baffled even his colleagues.
mnemonic: abs(떨어져) + truse(밀다, 라틴어 trudere) = 멀리 밀려난 → 난해한. trud/trus(밀다): intrude, obtrude

example: The king surrounded himself with sycophants who only told him what he wanted to hear.
mnemonic: 그리스어 sykophantes(무화과 고발자), sykon(무화과) + phant(보여주다). 무화과 밀수를 고발하는 밀고자 → 아첨꾼

example: The anomalous test results prompted further investigation.
mnemonic: a(없는) + nom(규칙) + alous = 규칙에 맞지 않는 → 변칙적인. nom(법칙): anomaly, autonomy

example: Her ebullience was contagious and lifted everyone's spirits.
mnemonic: e(밖으로) + bull(끓다, 라틴어 bullire) + ience = 끓어 넘치는 것 → 넘치는 열정. bull(끓다): ebullient, boil

example: The indulgent grandmother spoiled her grandchildren with gifts and sweets.
mnemonic: in(안에) + dulg(친절한, 라틴어 indulgere=허용하다) + ent = 안에서 허용하는 → 관대한. indulge(탐닉하다)

example: The company was forced to retrench by laying off hundreds of employees.
mnemonic: re(다시) + trench(자르다, 프랑스어 trancher) = 다시 잘라내다 → 절감하다. trench(자르다): trenchant

example: The artist is known for her whimsical illustrations of fantasy worlds.
mnemonic: whim(변덕) + sical = 변덕에 따른 → 변덕스러운, 기발한

example: The protean actor transformed himself completely for each new role.
mnemonic: Proteus(프로테우스, 그리스 신화의 변신하는 바다 신) + an = 프로테우스처럼 변하는 → 변화무쌍한

example: The insurance policy will indemnify the homeowner against flood damage.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + demn(손해, 라틴어 damnum) + ify = 손해가 아니게 하다 → 배상하다. damn(손해): damage, condemn, indemnity

example: The intrepid explorers ventured deep into the uncharted jungle.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + trepid(떨리는, 라틴어 trepidus) = 떨리지 않는 → 용감한. trepid(떨리는): trepidation

example: The sudden influx of refugees strained the city's resources.
mnemonic: in(안으로) + flux(흐름, 라틴어 fluxus) = 안으로 흐르는 → 유입. flu/flux(흐르다): fluid, fluent, affluent, confluence

example: The artist's provocative work sparked heated debate among critics.
mnemonic: pro(앞으로) + voc(부르다) + ative = 앞으로 불러내는 → 도발적인. voc(부르다): invoke, evoke, vocal

example: The company experienced a precipitous decline in sales last quarter.
mnemonic: pre(앞으로) + cipit(머리, 라틴어 caput) + ous = 머리부터 떨어지는 → 가파른. precipitate(촉발하다)와 같은 어근

example: The coach objurgated the players for their lack of effort in the game.
mnemonic: ob(향해) + jurg(싸우다, 라틴어 jurgare) + ate = 향해 싸우듯 → 호되게 꾸짖다

example: He gave a perfunctory nod without really paying attention to what was said.
mnemonic: per(완전히) + funct(수행하다) + ory = 그냥 수행만 하는 → 형식적인. funct(기능): function, defunct, malfunction

example: She coveted the beautiful necklace displayed in the jewelry store window.
mnemonic: 라틴어 cupiditas(탐욕), cupere(갈망하다)에서 유래. 강하게 갈망하다 → 탐내다. Cupid(큐피드=욕망의 신)

example: The so-called doctor turned out to be a charlatan with no medical degree.
mnemonic: 이탈리아어 ciarlatano(떠벌이)에서 유래. ciarlare(수다 떨다) → 말만 많고 실력 없는 사기꾼, 돌팔이

example: The renascent interest in classical music has led to sold-out concerts.
mnemonic: re(다시) + nasc(태어나다, 라틴어 nasci) + ent = 다시 태어나는 → 부활하는. nasc(태어나다): nascent, renaissance

example: A general malaise settled over the nation during the economic downturn.
mnemonic: mal(나쁜, 프랑스어) + aise(편안함) = 나쁜 편안함(=불편함) → 불쾌감. mal(나쁜): malady, malcontent

example: The senator vituperated his opponents in a fiery speech on the floor.
mnemonic: vit(결함, 라틴어 vitium) + uper(치다) + ate = 결함을 때리다 → 맹렬히 비난하다. vit(결함): vice, vitiate

example: There is a plethora of online courses available for self-study.
mnemonic: pleth(가득 찬, 그리스어 plethein) + ora = 가득 찬 것 → 과다

example: The philosopher criticized modern hedonism and its focus on material pleasures.
mnemonic: 그리스어 hedone(쾌락) + ism = 쾌락주의. hedon(쾌락): hedonist, hedonistic

example: The pernicious effects of misinformation threaten democratic institutions.
mnemonic: per(완전히) + nic(죽이다, 라틴어 necare) + ious = 완전히 죽이는 → 치명적인. nec/nic(죽이다): nectar, internecine

example: The plan was still inchoate and needed much more development.
mnemonic: in(안에) + choate(시작하다, 라틴어 inchoare) = 막 시작된 → 초기의, 미완성의

example: The new employees proved to be tractable and eager to learn.
mnemonic: tract(끌다, 라틴어 trahere) + able = 끌려갈 수 있는 → 다루기 쉬운. tract(끌다): attract, extract, subtract, tractor

example: He became despondent after failing the medical exam for the third time.
mnemonic: de(떨어져) + spond(약속하다, 라틴어 spondere) + ent = 약속(희망)이 떨어진 → 낙담한. spond(약속): respond, sponsor, spouse

example: The courtiers genuflected before the throne as the king entered the chamber.
mnemonic: genu(무릎, 라틴어) + flect(구부리다, 라틴어 flectere) = 무릎을 구부리다 → 무릎을 꿇다. flect(구부리다): reflect, deflect, inflect

example: The importunate salesman called every day until she finally agreed to a meeting.
mnemonic: im(향해) + portun(적절한, opportunus의 반대) + ate = 부적절하게 요구하는 → 집요한. importune(졸르다)과 같은 어근

example: The unctuous salesman's flattery was transparent and off-putting.
mnemonic: unct(기름, 라틴어 ungere=기름 바르다) + uous = 기름진 → 아첨하는. unct(기름): ointment, unguent

example: The witness signed an affidavit confirming her testimony.
mnemonic: af(~에게) + fid(믿다) + avit = 신뢰를 맹세한 것 → 선서 진술서. fid(믿다): confide, fidelity, confident

example: The lawyer's casuistry allowed him to justify almost any questionable action.
mnemonic: casu(경우, 라틴어 casus=사건) + istry = 개별 사례를 교묘히 따지는 것 → 궤변. casus(경우): case, casual, occasion

example: The court awarded pecuniary damages to the injured party.
mnemonic: pecuni(돈, 라틴어 pecunia=가축=재산) + ary = 돈에 관한 → 금전적인. 고대 로마에서 가축이 화폐 역할

example: She is fastidious about keeping her workspace perfectly organized.
mnemonic: '패스티디어스' → 'fast(빠른)+idious' 빠르게 결점을 찾아내는 까다로운!

example: The lawyer used legal chicanery to get his guilty client acquitted.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 chicaner(속이다, 궤변을 늘어놓다)에서 유래 → 속임수, 궤변

example: The two countries share a contiguous border stretching over 500 miles.
mnemonic: con(함께) + tig(접촉하다, 라틴어 tangere) + uous = 서로 접촉하는 → 인접한. tang(접촉): tangible, contact, contagious

example: His abrasive personality made him difficult to work with.
mnemonic: ab(떨어져) + ras(긁다, 라틴어 radere) + ive = 긁어내는 → 거친. ras/rad(긁다): erase, razor

example: The museum hosted a retrospective exhibition of the artist's fifty-year career.
mnemonic: retro(뒤로) + spect(보다) + ive = 뒤를 돌아보는 → 회고적인. spect(보다): inspect, prospect, spectacle

example: Her incisive analysis of the problem helped the team find a solution quickly.
mnemonic: '인사이시브' → 'incis(자르다)+ive' 날카롭게 자르는 예리한!

example: Critics dismissed the aristocracy as effete and out of touch with reality.
mnemonic: ef(밖으로) + fete(태어나다, 라틴어 fetus) = 출산을 다 한 → 쇠약한. fet(태어나다): fetus, fertile

example: The mathematical savant could solve complex equations in his head.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 savant(아는 사람), 라틴어 sapere(알다, 맛보다). 아는 사람 → 학자. sap(알다): sapient, savor

example: The solicitous nurse checked on the patient every hour throughout the night.
mnemonic: solic(전체, 라틴어 sollus) + it(움직이다) + ous = 전체를 돌보려 움직이는 → 걱정하는. solicit(간청하다)와 같은 어근

example: The invention of the printing press marked a new epoch in human history.
mnemonic: 그리스어 epokhe(멈추는 지점, epi+echein=멈추다). 시간이 멈추는 지점 → 획기적 시대

example: She expressed her thoughts with great felicity of language.
mnemonic: felic(행복한, 라틴어 felix) + ity = 행복한 상태 → 행복, 적절함. felicitous(적절한)의 명사형

example: The book is a paean to the beauty of the natural world.
mnemonic: 그리스어 paian(아폴로의 찬가). 신에게 바치는 찬양의 노래 → 찬가

example: British empiricism emphasizes sensory experience as the basis of knowledge.
mnemonic: empir(경험, 그리스어 empeiria) + icism = 경험에 기반한 주의 → 경험주의. empir(경험): empirical, empire(경험으로 다스림)

example: A schism within the party threatened to destroy its chances in the election.
mnemonic: 그리스어 schisma(갈라짐), schizein(가르다). 갈라짐 → 분열. schiz(가르다): schizophrenia

example: The vapid television show offered nothing of intellectual substance.
mnemonic: vap(김이 빠진, 라틴어 vapidus) + id = 김이 빠진 → 재미없는. vapor(증기)와 같은 어근: 증기가 빠진

example: The author wrote in a demotic style accessible to ordinary readers.
mnemonic: demo(민중, 그리스어 demos) + tic = 민중의 → 통속적인. demo(민중): democracy, demographic, epidemic
